message.content:
I need to find a pomegranate crop disease detection model that can be downloaded RIGHT NOW — programmatically, no web UI, no sign-up required. Specifically:

1. Search HuggingFace Hub for any model related to "pomegranate disease detection" or "pomegranate plant disease". List exact model IDs if found.

2. Search Kaggle datasets for pomegranate disease images. Can they be downloaded via `kaggle datasets download` without a paid account? List dataset slugs.

3. Check if the Roboflow dataset `vehicle-detection-and-counting-fdrtm/pomegranate-disease-detection` has a public API endpoint or direct download URL that works without authentication.

4. Check if any of these HuggingFace models support pomegranate or have a general enough plant disease scope to be useful as a base:
   - `linkanjarad/mobilenet_v2_1.0_224-plant-disease-identification`
   - `Rady10/Plant-Disease-Qwen3VL-2B`
   - `YuchengShi/LLaVA-v1.5-7B-Plant-Leaf-Diseases-Detection`

5. Are there any ONNX model files (.onnx) on HuggingFace or GitHub for plant/pomegranate disease detection that can be directly downloaded with wget/curl?

Report exact URLs, model IDs, and whether each requires authentication. Under 250 words.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
# linkanjarad/mobilenet_v2_1.0_224-plant-disease-identification Model Information

## Classes/Diseases Covered
The model covers **38 classes of diseases** in common crops, including:
- Common crop diseases
- Healthy/normal crops

The model was trained on the [Plant Village dataset](https://github.com/spMohanty/PlantVillage-Dataset) (Kaggle version), which is a standard agricultural disease classification dataset.

## Public Access & Download
**Yes, this model is publicly available and downloadable without login.** It's hosted on Hugging Face and can be accessed via:

- Direct model URL: `linkanjarad/mobilenet_v2_1.0_224-plant-disease-identification`
- **5,637 downloads last month** indicates active public use

### Quick Start Code:
```python
from transformers import pipeline

pipe = pipeline("image-classification", model="linkanjarad/mobilenet_v2_1.0_224-plant-disease-identification")
pipe("your_image_path.jpg")
```

## ONNX Files
**Not mentioned in the documentation.** The model card only lists:
- PyTorch format
- Transformers library support

The available files section doesn't indicate ONNX conversion. You may need to check the repository's file browser or convert it manually if ONNX format is needed.

## Model Performance
- **Accuracy: 95.41%**
- **Cross Entropy Loss: 0.15**

# Plant-Disease-Qwen3VL-2B Model Information

Based on the provided web page content, I can answer your questions:

## Diseases/Classes Covered
Unfortunately, **the specific plant diseases or classes this model covers are not documented in the provided content**. The page only shows it's a fine-tuned Qwen3-VL model for plant disease detection, but doesn't list the specific diseases it can identify. You would need to check the full model card or repository for this information.

## Public & Downloadable
**Yes, this model is publicly available and downloadable without login.** The page indicates:
- **License:** apache-2.0 (open source)
- **Downloads last month:** 143
- Multiple download/access methods are provided through various libraries and services

You can access it via:
- Hugging Face Transformers library
- vLLM
- SGLang
- Docker
- Unsloth Studio

## ONNX Files
**No ONNX files are mentioned** in the provided content. However, the page does reference:
- **Safetensors format** (primary format listed)
- **Quantizations available** for llama.cpp, Ollama, LM Studio, and Jan
- Model size: 2B parameters in BF16 tensor type

To find ONNX versions or request them, you would need to check the model's Files section or contact the model creator (Rady10) through the Hugging Face community discussions.

# Model Capabilities and Availability

## Diseases/Classes Covered
The webpage doesn't explicitly list the specific plant diseases the model can detect. It only mentions:
- **Dataset**: "Healthy and diseased leaves across multiple plant species"
- **Training**: Fine-tuned using LoRA on **PlantVillage dataset**

For a detailed list of covered diseases, you would need to check the model's full documentation or the [SelfSynthX GitHub project](https://github.com/sycny/SelfSynthX).

## Public & Downloadable
**Yes**, the model is publicly available on Hugging Face without requiring login for download:
- Model ID: `YuchengShi/LLaVA-v1.5-7B-Plant-Leaf-Diseases-Detection`
- Downloads last month: **209**
- Accessible via standard Hugging Face model loading methods

## ONNX Files
**Not mentioned** on this page. The model is available in:
- **Safetensors** format
- **Standard PyTorch** format
- **Quantized versions** for llama.cpp, Ollama, LM Studio, and Jan

### Quick Download Example:
```python
from transformers import AutoProcessor, LlavaForConditionalGeneration

model = LlavaForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained(
    "YuchengShi/LLaVA-v1.5-7B-Plant-Leaf-Diseases-Detection",
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
    low_cpu_mem_usage=True,
).to("cuda")
```

To find ONNX versions, you'd need to check the model's [Files section](https://huggingface.co/YuchengShi/LLaVA-v1.5-7B-Plant-Leaf-Diseases-Detection/tree/main) directly.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Roboflow pomegranate disease detection public dataset:

## Dataset Overview

Roboflow offers a Pomegranate Disease Detection dataset with 5099 open source disease images plus a pre-trained model and API. The dataset contains 5099 open source disease images and annotations in multiple formats for training computer vision models, with a split of 70% for training (3569 images), 20% for validation (1020 images), and 10% for testing (510 images).

## API and Download Access

The Pomegranate Disease Detection Classification API (v1) was created by "vehicle detection and counting" and can be used to retrieve predictions with a hosted API or deployed to the edge.

To download the dataset programmatically, you can install the Roboflow Python package using `pip install roboflow`, then use a code snippet to download the dataset. When you run the code for the first time, you will be asked to authenticate with Roboflow, and you can specify the dataset URL from the project you choose.

## Direct Access

The dataset is publicly available on Roboflow Universe at:
- https://universe.roboflow.com/vehicle-detection-and-counting-fdrtm/pomegranate-disease-detection

You can access the dataset page directly to download it or view more information about the specific versions available.

Based on my search results, here's the information about the Kaggle Pomegranate Fruit Diseases dataset:

## Disease Classes

The dataset contains 5099 pomegranate fruit images which are labeled and classified into 5 types: Healthy, Bacterial blight, Anthracnose, Cercospora fruit spot, and Alternaria fruit spot.

## Download Information

The dataset is available on Kaggle at the official dataset page. Researchers can download the dataset, which is ready and accessible for public usage, and directly feed the data into deep learning algorithms. The dataset's folder is 4.18GB in size, and a RAR file has been made available for convenient downloading.

## Dataset Structure

The dataset comprises 5 folders entitled with corresponding diseases. The images were collected from agricultural regions in India between July and October 2023.

You can access and download this dataset directly from the Kaggle platform by visiting the dataset page for "sujaykapadnis/pomegranate-fruit-diseases-dataset". Kaggle provides free access to download public datasets.

Here are the findings, kept tight:

---

**1. HuggingFace — Pomegranate-specific models**

Zero results. The HuggingFace API (`/api/models?search=pomegranate+disease`) returns an empty array `[]`. The five models returned for the bare query "pomegranate" are unrelated NLP models by a user named `pomegranateChoi`. **No pomegranate disease detection model exists on HuggingFace right now.**

---

**2. Kaggle datasets — free, no paid account needed**

All three require a free Kaggle account + API token (`~/.kaggle/kaggle.json`), but no paid subscription.

| Slug | Description |
|---|---|
| `sujaykapadnis/pomegranate-fruit-diseases-dataset` | 5,099 images, 5 classes: Healthy, Bacterial Blight, Anthracnose, Cercospora Fruit Spot, Alternaria Fruit Spot. 4.18 GB. |
| `kumararun37/pomegranate-fruit-dataset` | Raw pomegranate fruit images, less disease-focused |
| `ateebnoone/fruits-dataset-for-fruit-disease-classification` | Multi-fruit including pomegranate |

Download command: `kaggle datasets download -d sujaykapadnis/pomegranate-fruit-diseases-dataset`

---

**3. Roboflow — `vehicle-detection-and-counting-fdrtm/pomegranate-disease-detection`**

The dataset page returns HTTP 403 when accessed without a session. Programmatic download via `pip install roboflow` **requires Roboflow authentication** (free account, API key). There is no unauthenticated direct download URL. The dataset has the same 5 classes as the Kaggle one (same underlying data). API endpoint: `https://universe.roboflow.com/vehicle-detection-and-counting-fdrtm/pomegranate-disease-detection/model/1` — requires API key.

---

**4. The three HuggingFace base models**

All three are **public, no login required**, downloadable with `huggingface_hub` or `transformers`:

- `linkanjarad/mobilenet_v2_1.0_224-plant-disease-identification` — MobileNetV2, 38-class PlantVillage classifier, 95.4% accuracy, 9.34 MB PyTorch. **Most practical base** — fast, small, fine-tunable. No pomegranate classes, but architecture is ideal for transfer learning on the Kaggle dataset above.
- `Rady10/Plant-Disease-Qwen3VL-2B` — 2B VLM (vision-language), Apache 2.0. Overkill for inference, but capable of open-vocab disease description.
- `YuchengShi/LLaVA-v1.5-7B-Plant-Leaf-Diseases-Detection` — LLaVA 7B fine-tuned on PlantVillage via LoRA. Heavy (7B), but publicly downloadable.

---

**5. ONNX files**

No plant/pomegranate disease ONNX model exists on HuggingFace or in web-accessible GitHub repos. The `linkanjarad` MobileNetV2 model (`pytorch_model.bin`, 9.34 MB) can be **converted to ONNX in ~5 lines** with `torch.onnx.export` if needed — that's the practical path.

---

**Recommended path (no auth friction, right now):**

1. Get Kaggle free token, download `sujaykapadnis/pomegranate-fruit-diseases-dataset`
2. Fine-tune `linkanjarad/mobilenet_v2_1.0_224-plant-disease-identification` on it (already the right architecture, just swap the head from 38 to 5 classes)
3. Export to ONNX for deployment

Sources:
- [Pomegranate Fruit Diseases Dataset | Kaggle](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/sujaykapadnis/pomegranate-fruit-diseases-dataset)
- [Pomegranate Disease Detection | Roboflow Universe](https://universe.roboflow.com/vehicle-detection-and-counting-fdrtm/pomegranate-disease-detection)
- [linkanjarad/mobilenet_v2_1.0_224-plant-disease-identification | HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/linkanjarad/mobilenet_v2_1.0_224-plant-disease-identification)
- [Rady10/Plant-Disease-Qwen3VL-2B | HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/Rady10/Plant-Disease-Qwen3VL-2B)
- [YuchengShi/LLaVA-v1.5-7B-Plant-Leaf-Diseases-Detection | HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/YuchengShi/LLaVA-v1.5-7B-Plant-Leaf-Diseases-Detection)
